behavior.name: git-conventions
behavior.summary: All git rules in one place: branching model, branch naming, commit format, and push discipline. Every agent that touches git must follow these rules.

section.name: branching-model
section.rule: All development is trunk-based. Every ticket gets its own short-lived branch off the base branch and merges back. Branches are deleted after merge.

The base branch determines where ticket branches are created from and where pull requests target: - "main" (default): ticket branches are created from main, PRs merge to main. - A feature branch (EPIC/{number}-{desc}): created from main for multi-ticket work. Ticket branches are created from the feature branch, PRs merge to the feature branch. A final PR merges the feature branch to main after all children complete.
The epic ticket is closed only after all children are closed. When using a feature branch, the epic is closed as part of the final pull request from the feature branch to main.

section.name: branch-naming
section.rule: All commits happen on a ticket branch or feature branch, never directly on main

section.name: add-on-labels
section.rule: Add-on labels are optional tags applied to GitHub issues alongside their issue type label. They do NOT affect branch naming or commit format — those are determined solely by the issue's type label.
section.label: Signals that the issue will be executed by an AI agent running the full workflow in autonomous mode with no human at the gates. Applied via: gh issue edit {number} --add-label "autonomously-nightly"

section.name: commit-discipline
section.rule: Every commit message starts with #{number} referencing the GitHub issue being worked. Always use the child issue number, not the epic number.
Commits must be granular — one logical change per commit
Multiple commits per sub-task are expected
Each commit must be atomic and independently valid (builds, no broken imports)
Never commit secrets, .env files, or credentials
Always verify the build passes before the final commit of a task

section.name: push-discipline
section.rule: Only one agent pushes to the remote, and only after human approval. This ensures unapproved work never reaches the remote.
After human approval following implementation: the branch is pushed to remote. This is a single push covering all commits.
After human approval before opening the pull request: any remaining commits (from documentation updates) are pushed and the pull request is created.
Never force-push unless the human explicitly requests it
